title: Xtra Sour German Oyster Mushroom Pilaf
description: A quick and sour German dish featuring oyster mushrooms and a pillowy texture, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This dish is nut-free and has an al-dente finish.
instructions: Rinse the oyster mushrooms and pat them dry with a paper towel.
In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat and saute the diced onion until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the oyster mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the white vinegar, chicken broth, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until the liquid has been absorbed and the mushrooms are tender, about 10 minutes.
If using white wine, stir it in during the last 2 minutes of cooking.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
